text.title: ts-strict-isolated Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ts-strict-isolated. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every tsconfig.json sets compilerOptions.strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true. Library tsconfigs additionally set isolatedDeclarations: true and composite: true.
rule.rationale: strict enables noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, strictFunctionTypes, etc. — the baseline. noUncheckedIndexedAccess forces narrowing on arr[i] and obj[key], the #1 LLM blind spot. exactOptionalPropertyTypes separates "missing key" from "key set to undefined", killing partial-update bugs. isolatedDeclarations requires explicit return types on every export so non-tsc tools can emit .d.ts.

rule.statement: Every library package's tsconfig sets composite: true and lists every internal dependency under references: [{ path: "../<dep>" }]. Application packages add references to all libraries they import. Run tsc --build from the root; never tsc -p per package in CI.
rule.rationale: Without composite, declaration emit is incremental-incompatible and editor navigation re-checks across boundaries. With it, each package builds once, downstream packages reuse .d.ts, total CI time drops 3–10x on large monorepos. tsc --build orchestrates the dependency graph; ad-hoc per-package builds skip the graph and rebuild redundantly.
